bastions.name: Bedroom
bastions.description: A basic facility with nonmagical furnishings and decor appropriate for sleeping quarters.

bastions.name: Courtyard
bastions.description: A basic facility with nonmagical furnishings and decor appropriate for an open-air space.

bastions.name: Dining Room
bastions.description: A basic facility with nonmagical furnishings and decor appropriate for dining.

bastions.name: Kitchen
bastions.description: A basic facility with nonmagical furnishings and decor appropriate for preparing food.

bastions.name: Parlor
bastions.description: A basic facility with nonmagical furnishings and decor appropriate for social gatherings.

bastions.name: Storage
bastions.description: A basic facility with nonmagical furnishings and decor appropriate for storing goods.

bastions.name: Arcane Study
bastions.prerequisite: Ability to use an Arcane Focus or a tool as a Spellcasting Focus
bastions.description:
An Arcane Study is a place of quiet research that contains one or more desks and bookshelves.

Arcane Study Charm. After spending a Long Rest in your Bastion, you gain a magical Charm that lasts for 7 days or until you use it. The Charm allows you to cast Identify without expending a spell slot or using Material components. You can’t gain this Charm again while you still have it.

Craft Options:
- Craft: Arcane Focus. Work takes 7 days and costs no money.
- Craft: Book. Work takes 7 days and costs 10 GP.
- Craft: Magic Item (Arcana). If you are level 9+, a hireling can craft a Common or Uncommon magic item from the Arcana tables, using chapter 7 crafting rules.

bastions.name: Armory
bastions.description:
An Armory contains mannequins for displaying armor, hooks for holding shields, racks for storing weapons, and chests for holding ammunition.

Trade: Stock Armory. Costs 100 GP plus an extra 100 GP for each Bastion Defender. If your Bastion has a Smithy, total cost is halved.

While stocked, Bastion Defenders are harder to kill during events: roll 1d8 in place of each d6 when determining defender losses. Stock is expended after the event.

bastions.name: Barrack
bastions.description:
A Barrack serves as sleeping quarters for up to twelve Bastion Defenders. A Bastion can have more than one.

Recruit: Bastion Defenders. Each Recruit order adds up to four Bastion Defenders at no cost, provided the Barrack is not full.

Enlarging the Facility. You can enlarge your Barrack to a Vast facility by spending 2,000 GP. A Vast Barrack can accommodate up to twenty-five Bastion Defenders.

bastions.name: Garden
bastions.description:
A Bastion can have more than one Garden. Each Garden has a type: Decorative, Food, Herb, or Poison.

Harvest: Garden Growth. Work takes 7 days and costs no money.

Garden Types:
- Decorative: ten floral bouquets worth 5 GP each, ten vials of perfume, or ten candles
- Food: 100 days worth of rations
- Herb: herbs for ten healer’s kits or one potion of healing
- Poison: plants for two vials of antitoxin or one vial of basic poison

A Garden can be changed to another type in 21 days. Enlarging to Vast costs 2,000 GP; a Vast Garden counts as two Roomy Gardens and gains one additional hireling.

bastions.name: Library
bastions.description:
A Library contains a collection of books plus one or more desks and reading chairs.

Research: Topical Lore. Work takes 7 days. The hireling learns up to three accurate pieces of information about a topic previously unknown to you. The DM determines the information learned.

bastions.name: Sanctuary
bastions.prerequisite: Ability to use a Holy Symbol or Druidic Focus as a Spellcasting Focus
bastions.description:
Icons of your religion are displayed in this facility, which includes a quiet place for worship.

Sanctuary Charm. After spending a Long Rest in your Bastion, you gain a magical Charm that allows you to cast Healing Word once without expending a spell slot. The Charm lasts 7 days or until used.

Craft: Sacred Focus. Work takes 7 days and costs no money. Produces a Druidic Focus (wooden staff) or Holy Symbol.

bastions.name: Smithy
bastions.description:
This Smithy contains a forge, an anvil, and tools needed to craft weapons, armor, and other equipment.

Craft Options:
- Craft: Smith’s Tools. Craft anything made with Smith’s Tools using Player’s Handbook rules.
- Craft: Magic Item (Armament). If you are level 9+, hirelings can craft a Common or Uncommon magic item from the Armaments tables, using chapter 7 crafting rules.

bastions.name: Storehouse
bastions.description:
A Storehouse is a cool, dark space meant to contain trade goods and other nonmagical items.

Trade: Goods. Over 7 days, the hireling either procures nonmagical items up to a value cap or sells goods in storage. Value caps increase with level:
- 500 GP base
- 2,000 GP at level 9
- 5,000 GP at level 13

Sale profit bonus:
- 10% base
- 20% at level 9
- 50% at level 13
- 100% at level 17

bastions.name: Workshop
bastions.description:
This Workshop is a creative space where useful items can be crafted. It comes equipped with six different kinds of Artisan’s Tools.

Craft Options:
- Craft: Adventuring Gear. Craft anything that can be made with the tools chosen for the Workshop.
- Craft: Magic Item (Implement). If you are level 9+, hirelings can craft a Common or Uncommon magic item from the Implements tables, using chapter 7 crafting rules.

Source of Inspiration. After spending an entire Short Rest in your Workshop, you gain Heroic Inspiration. You can’t gain this again until you finish a Long Rest.

Enlarging the Facility. Enlarging to Vast costs 2,000 GP and adds two hirelings and three additional Artisan’s Tools.

bastions.name: Gaming Hall
bastions.description:
A Gaming Hall offers recreational activities like chess and games of darts, cards, or dice.

Trade: Gambling Hall. Over 7 days, the hall becomes a gambling den. At the end, roll 1d100 to determine winnings:
01–50: 1d6 × 10 GP
51–85: 2d6 × 10 GP
86–95: 4d6 × 10 GP
96–00: 10d6 × 10 GP

bastions.name: Greenhouse
bastions.description:
A Greenhouse is an enclosure where rare plants and fungi are nurtured in a controlled climate.

Fruit of Restoration. One plant grows three magical fruits. Each fruit grants the effect of Lesser Restoration. Fruits lose magic within 24 hours of being picked. The plant regrows them daily at dawn.

Harvest Options:
- Harvest: Healing Herbs. Create a Potion of Healing (greater) in 7 days at no cost.
- Harvest: Poison. Extract one application of Assassin’s Blood, Malice, Pale Tincture, or Truth Serum in 7 days at no cost.

bastions.name: Laboratory
bastions.description:
A Laboratory contains storage space for alchemical supplies and workspaces for crafting concoctions.

Craft Options:
- Craft: Alchemist’s Supplies. Craft anything made with Alchemist’s Supplies.
- Craft: Poison. Craft Burnt Othur Fumes, Essence of Ether, or Torpor. Work takes 7 days and costs half the poison’s listed cost.

*Certain orders have additional prerequisites.

bastions.name: Sacristy
bastions.prerequisite: Ability to use a Holy Symbol or Druidic Focus as a Spellcasting Focus
bastions.description:
A Sacristy serves as a preparation and storage room for sacred items and religious vestments.

Craft Options:
- Craft: Holy Water. Work takes 7 days and costs no money. You may spend up to 500 GP to increase its damage by 1d8 per 100 GP.
- Craft: Magic Item (Relic). Craft a Common or Uncommon magic item from the Relics tables, using chapter 7 crafting rules.

Spell Refreshment. After an entire Short Rest in your Bastion, you can regain one expended spell slot of level 5 or lower. You can’t gain this benefit again until you finish a Long Rest.

bastions.name: Scriptorium
bastions.description:
A Scriptorium contains desks and writing supplies.

Craft Options:
- Craft: Book Replica. Copy a nonmagical book using a blank book. Work takes 7 days.
- Craft: Spell Scroll. Scribe a Cleric or Wizard Spell Scroll of level 3 or lower.
- Craft: Paperwork. Create up to fifty copies of a broadsheet, pamphlet, or similar loose-leaf product in 7 days at 1 GP per copy, with optional distribution within 50 miles.

*Certain orders have additional prerequisites.

bastions.name: Stable
bastions.description:
A Bastion can have more than one Stable. Each Stable starts with one Riding Horse or Camel and two Ponies or Mules. It houses three Large animals, or equivalent smaller creatures.

After a Beast that can serve as a mount spends at least 14 days here, Animal Handling checks regarding it have Advantage.

Trade: Animals. Over 7 days, buy or sell mounts at normal cost. Sale profit bonus:
- 20% base
- 50% at level 13
- 100% at level 17

Enlarging the Facility. Enlarging to Vast costs 2,000 GP and allows housing up to six Large animals.

bastions.name: Teleportation Circle
bastions.description:
This room contains a permanent teleportation circle.

Recruit: Spellcaster. Roll any die. On odd, the Friendly NPC spellcaster declines. On even, they arrive via the circle.

While you are in your Bastion, you can ask the spellcaster to cast one Wizard spell:
- level 4 or lower normally
- level 8 or lower at level 17+

You must pay costly Material components. The spellcaster stays 14 days or until they cast a spell for you.

bastions.name: Theater
bastions.description:
The Theater contains a stage, backstage area, and seating for a small audience.

Empower: Theatrical Event. Rehearsals take 14 days, followed by at least 7 days of performances.

Characters can contribute as Composer/Writer, Conductor/Director, or Performer. At the end of rehearsals, contributors make DC 15 Charisma (Performance) checks. If more succeed than fail, each contributor gains a Theater die:
- d6 base
- d8 at level 13
- d10 at level 17

The die can be added to one D20 Test after rolling.

bastions.name: Training Area
bastions.description:
A Bastion can have more than one Training Area. One hireling is an expert trainer and the others are training partners.

Trainer types:
- Battle Expert
- Skills Expert
- Tools Expert
- Unarmed Combat Expert
- Weapon Expert

Empower: Training. Over 7 days, any character who trains here at least 8 hours each day gains the benefit associated with the current trainer for 7 days.

bastions.name: Trophy Room
bastions.description:
This room houses mementos, trophies, weapons, and trinkets.

Research Options:
- Research: Lore. Over 7 days, the hireling learns up to three accurate pieces of information about a topic.
- Research: Trinket Trophy. Over 7 days, roll any die. On even, the hireling finds a Common magic item from the Implements table. On odd, nothing useful is found.

bastions.name: Archive
bastions.description:
An Archive is a repository of valuable books, maps, and scrolls, often attached to a Library.

Research: Helpful Lore. Work takes 7 days. The hireling gains knowledge as if they had cast Legend Lore, then shares it with you.

Reference Book. The Archive contains one rare reference book that grants Advantage on a corresponding Intelligence study action in your Bastion.

Enlarging the Facility. Enlarging to Vast costs 2,000 GP and grants two additional reference books.

bastions.name: Meditation Chamber
bastions.description:
A Meditation Chamber is a relaxing space that helps align mind, body, and spirit.

Empower: Inner Peace. The next time you roll for a Bastion event, you can roll twice and choose either result.

Fortify Self. If you meditate here for 7 straight days without leaving the Bastion, you gain Advantage on two randomly determined saving throw types for the next 7 days.

bastions.name: Menagerie
bastions.description:
A Menagerie has enclosures big enough to contain up to four Large creatures. Four Small or Medium creatures can occupy the same space as one Large creature.

Recruit: Creature. In 7 days, add a creature from the Menagerie Creatures table by paying its listed cost.

Creatures in the Menagerie count as Bastion Defenders unless you decide otherwise.

bastions.name: Observatory
bastions.prerequisite: Ability to use a Spellcasting Focus
bastions.description:
Situated atop your Bastion, your Observatory contains a telescope aimed at the night sky.

Observatory Charm. After a Long Rest here, you gain a Charm allowing you to cast Contact Other Plane once without expending a spell slot. The Charm lasts 7 days or until used.

Empower: Eldritch Discovery. Over 7 consecutive nights, explore the mysteries of the stars. At the end, roll a die; on odd, grant one Charm of Darkvision, Heroism, or Vitality to a creature on the same plane.

bastions.name: Pub
bastions.description:
A Pub is a social space such as a bar, coffee shop, or tea room. The bartender maintains a spy network.

Research: Information Gathering. Over the next 7 days, spies report important events within 10 miles and can reveal the location of a familiar creature within 50 miles, if not protected by magic or beyond their reach.

Pub Special. The Pub has one magical beverage on tap, chosen from:
- Bigby’s Burden
- Kiss of the Spider Queen
- Moonlight Serenade
- Positive Reinforcement
- Sterner Stuff

Enlarging the Facility. Enlarging to Vast costs 2,000 GP, allows two magical beverages on tap at once, and increases hirelings to 4.

bastions.name: Reliquary
bastions.prerequisite: Ability to use a Holy Symbol or Druidic Focus as a Spellcasting Focus
bastions.description:
This vault holds sacred objects.

Reliquary Charm. After spending a Long Rest in your Bastion, you gain a magical Charm that allows you to cast Greater Restoration once without expending a spell slot or Material components. The Charm lasts 7 days or until used.

Harvest: Talisman. In 7 days and at no cost, the hireling prepares a Tiny, nonmagical object of religious significance. It can replace one spell’s Material components costing 1,000 GP or less, and is not consumed if the spell normally consumes them. After use, it must be returned to the Reliquary and prepared again with another Harvest order.

bastions.name: Demiplane
bastions.prerequisite: Ability to use an Arcane Focus or a tool as a Spellcasting Focus
bastions.description:
A door appears on a flat, solid surface in one of your Bastion’s other facilities and leads to an extradimensional stone room. Only you and Bastion hirelings can open it.

Empower: Arcane Resilience. For 7 days, magical runes grant you Temporary Hit Points equal to five times your level after an entire Long Rest in the Demiplane.

Fabrication. Once per Long Rest, while in the Demiplane, you can create a small nonmagical object worth no more than 5 GP from allowed materials.

bastions.name: Guildhall
bastions.description:
A Guildhall comes with a guild that you lead as guild master. Your guild has roughly fifty members who live and work outside the Bastion.

Recruit: Guild Assignment. Assign guild members to a special task. Sample guilds include Adventurers’, Bakers’, Brewers’, Masons’, Shipbuilders’, and Thieves’ Guilds, each with a specific assignment outcome.

bastions.name: Sanctum
bastions.prerequisite: Ability to use a Holy Symbol or Druidic Focus as a Spellcasting Focus
bastions.description:
A Sanctum is a place of solace and healing.

Sanctum Charm. After a Long Rest in your Bastion, you gain a magical Charm allowing you to cast Heal once without a spell slot. The Charm lasts 7 days or until used.

Empower: Fortifying Rites. For 7 days, the beneficiary of the rites gains Temporary Hit Points equal to your level after each Long Rest.

Sanctum Recall. You always have Word of Recall prepared, and the Sanctum can be its destination. One creature arriving there via the spell also gains the benefit of Heal.

bastions.name: War Room
bastions.prerequisite: Fighting Style feature or Unarmored Defense feature
bastions.description:
The War Room is where you plan military actions with loyal lieutenants. You start with two Warrior Veteran lieutenants and can have up to ten.

Lieutenants reduce defender-loss dice during Bastion attacks if housed in the Bastion.

Recruit Options:
- Recruit: Lieutenant. Gain one new lieutenant, up to ten total.
- Recruit: Soldiers. Each lieutenant can muster one hundred Guards in 7 days, or twenty mounted Guards. Upkeep is 1 GP per day per guard and horse. The army disbands if unfed, leaderless, destroyed, or dismissed.
